evals.prompt: All PRs merged and CI passed. Close the sprint.
evals.expected_output: Refuses closure until exact revision deployment, runtime checks, and human outcome review are recorded.
assertions: Merge and deployment are distinguished.
Observed runtime revision is verified.
Outcome acceptance remains a separate step.

evals.prompt: Staging is healthy but reports a SHA different from the integrated release.
evals.expected_output: Marks verification failed and follows rollback/incident policy rather than accepting health alone.
assertions: The revision mismatch is blocking.
Health alone is insufficient.
Rollback or incident evidence is recorded.

evals.prompt: State-of-union needs live deployment and recent log evidence before it can replan the sprint.
evals.expected_output: Runs observability-diagnostics mode, records runtime identity, log/metric/trace/probe findings, missing instrumentation, and routes evidence back to state-of-union or a tracked issue.
assertions: Diagnostic evidence distinguishes runtime identity from behavior.
Logs and telemetry limitations are explicit.
Findings route to state_of_union, sprint_planning, issue creation, incident, or no action.

evals.prompt: A lane claims review-ready status, but the critic report names SHA aaa111, the PR head is bbb222, required checks ran on ccc333, and the preview deployment cannot report its revision.
evals.expected_output: Marks the review inbox packet blocked or incomplete, refuses an approve recommendation, records the SHA mismatches and missing runtime identity, and routes back to fix lane, hold, or fresh review.
assertions: The exact reviewed head SHA must match the PR, critic report, checks, and preview or review deployment before review-ready status can pass.
Missing observed runtime revision is blocking when preview or review deployment evidence is required.
Required check results are tied to the current head SHA with evidence URLs or observed timestamps, not summarized as CI passed.
The recommendation is request_changes, reject, escalate, or hold rather than approve.

evals.prompt: A lane was critic-approved yesterday, then a worker pushed a new commit to the PR branch before integration started.
evals.expected_output: Treats the prior critic approval and checks as stale for the new head SHA, revalidates current checks and critic approval, and blocks integration until current evidence exists.
assertions: Integration uses critic-approved head SHAs only.
Any new commit after critic approval invalidates prior approval for integration until fresh review or policy-approved revalidation occurs.
Required checks are re-read for the current PR head SHA before merge order is finalized.
The integration session records the stale evidence and does not merge based on obsolete approval.

evals.prompt: A worker lane has merged and asks to use production credentials from its session to deploy and verify the release immediately.
evals.expected_output: Refuses worker self-deployment, requires separately authorized release-verification deployment approval and scoped credentials, and keeps deployment verification distinct from merge success.
assertions: Worker lane credentials or session authority are not used for production deployment.
Deployment approval comes through the authorized environment or policy before any deploy action.
The exact integrated revision or artifact identity is verified separately from merge or CI success.
Outcome acceptance remains separate from worker closeout and merge state.
